content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Reviewed the branch diff against `master` and read the key iterative-minimal implementation files, including `src/shared/domain.rs`, `src/contexts/workflow_composition/{mod,engine}.rs`, `src/contexts/project_run_record/{model,journal,service}.rs`, `src/contexts/workspace_governance/config.rs`, `src/cli/run.rs`, and the related tests. I also ran targeted coverage for the new flow (`cargo test iterative_minimal`, `cargo test --features test-stub --test unit workflow_engine_test::iterative_minimal`, and `cargo test --features test-stub --test unit minimal_flow_remains_single_pass_without_iteration_events`). One fix-now robustness issue was found in iterative failure journaling.

## Amendments

1. finding_summary: Failed `iterative_minimal` iterations are journaled under the wrong invocation id. `execute_stage_with_retry()` always emits `stage_failed` with `history_record_base_id(...)` (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:5782`), but iterative implementer calls are actually invoked with suffixed ids from `invocation_id_for_stage(..., Some("it{iteration}"))` (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:7103`). When an implementer iteration fails, `stage_failed.details.invocation_id` and the matching runtime log entry no longer identify the real raw output artifact for that failure, which breaks failure forensics and any tooling/operators that follow the recorded invocation id to inspect the backend output. remediation_hint: thread the actual invocation id (or the iteration suffix) back out of the iterative invocation path so the failure event records the same id the backend used.
   Rationale: Per-iteration observability is part of this bead’s deliverable. Successful iterations already persist sidecars under suffixed ids, so recording an unsuffixed id on failure leaves the new workflow with misleading metadata precisely in the cases where debugging and resume triage are needed.
